entries.content: The reception is inspired by the Polaris star — the North Star that never moves while everything else rotates around it. Look up at the ceiling and you will see large circular light installations inspired by long-exposure photographs of the night sky, where the stars appear to trace infinite circles around a single fixed point. That fixed point is Polaris. This campus is designed to be exactly that — your constant reference point as you navigate your career in technology.
entries.short: You are at the reception — inspired by the Polaris star, your fixed point for navigation.
entries.experience_script: Welcome to Polaris School of Technology. The circular lights above you are inspired by long-exposure photographs of the night sky — where every star traces a circle around one fixed point: Polaris, the North Star. This campus is designed to be that for you — a constant, reliable guide as you build your future in technology.

entries.content: The merchandise display in the lobby is a living archive of the campus journey. Each item — a t-shirt from a Google hackathon, a bottle from a Microsoft mixer, a magazine from a key event — represents a real moment in time. This is not a trophy cabinet. It is a collection of memories, partnerships, and milestones, gradually building a legacy that future students will look back at to understand the culture they are stepping into.
entries.short: The merchandise display tells the story of Polaris through objects — each item is a real campus memory.
entries.experience_script: Each item in this display represents a moment in Polaris history — a hackathon won, a company visited, a speaker who changed how students think. Over time, this becomes a living archive of everything this campus has built and everyone who has been part of the journey.

entries.content: The Radial Classroom is a semi-circular learning space inspired by the great lecture halls of top global universities. Every seat has a direct, unobstructed view of the speaker and all screens — the design removes hierarchy between audience and presenter. This room is reserved for high-impact sessions: fireside chats, guest lectures, talks by founders and industry leaders. The acoustics and display systems are built to international standard so that both students and visiting speakers have a seamless, world-class experience.
entries.short: The Radial Classroom is a semi-circular lecture space built for fireside chats and high-impact talks.
entries.experience_script: This is the Radial Classroom. The semi-circular layout means every student has an equal, unobstructed view — no bad seats. This room hosts fireside chats, founder talks, and guest lectures. The acoustics are designed so that every word lands clearly, whether you are in the front row or the last.

entries.content: The Creator Studio is a professional-grade recording space built specifically for students. It has high-quality audio and video recording equipment, acoustic treatment for clean sound, a proper recording environment, and a prep area. The idea is to remove all friction between a student and their content. Whether you want to build a personal brand, teach what you know, document your journey, or create in any format — the tools and space are here, no booking required during open hours.
entries.short: The Creator Studio is a professional recording space built so students have zero friction to create content.
entries.experience_script: You have just entered the Creator Studio. This is a fully equipped recording space — acoustically treated, professionally lit, with high-quality audio and video gear — built entirely for students. The philosophy here is simple: if you want to build in public and share your thinking with the world, this campus will not let tools or space be the reason you do not.

entries.content: Just outside the Creator Studio is the Creator Lounge — a relaxed, informal space for brainstorming, script writing, collaboration, and preparation before recording sessions. Students can hang out here, refine ideas, and mentally prepare before stepping into the studio. The lounge and studio together form a dedicated creative ecosystem on campus.
entries.short: The Creator Lounge is the informal space outside the studio for brainstorming and prep.
entries.experience_script: This is the Creator Lounge — the thinking space before the making space. Students come here to brainstorm ideas, write scripts, and collaborate before stepping into the studio next door.

entries.content: The Mentor Room is a quiet, access-controlled space reserved for faculty and mentors. It is designed to give mentors space to prepare, reflect, and reset between sessions. Great teaching requires calm and preparation — by keeping this zone separate from student-heavy areas, the campus ensures mentors can show up fully present for every session.
entries.short: The Mentor Room is a private preparation space reserved for faculty. It is access controlled.
entries.experience_script: This is the Mentor Room — a private, quiet space reserved for faculty to prepare and reset. It is intentionally separated from the rest of the floor so that mentors can show up fully present for every session.

entries.content: The Arcade is a deliberate design decision. After long hours of coding and focused academic work, students need a space to completely switch off. This area has modern arcade setups alongside nostalgic 90s-style games, air hockey, and basketball — neon-lit and intentionally different in energy from everything else on this floor. The goal is mental reset through play. High performance in technology is supported by spaces that let you step away from screens, move your body, and reconnect with others informally.
entries.short: The Arcade is a neon-lit game zone designed for mental reset after intense academic sessions.
entries.experience_script: Welcome to the Arcade. This space exists for one reason: after hours of deep work and problem-solving, your brain needs to completely switch gears. Neon lights, classic arcade games, air hockey, basketball. No screens, no code — just play. This is not wasted time. This is how high performers recharge.

entries.content: The cafeteria is designed to feel like a cozy café, not a college canteen. Warm yellow lighting is used intentionally — it calms the nervous system and creates a sense of comfort. The goal is for students to slow down, actually enjoy their meals, and feel at home. This is a comfort space designed to recharge students emotionally, not just physically.
entries.short: The cafeteria is designed like a warm café — intentional yellow lighting to help you slow down and recharge.
entries.experience_script: This is the cafeteria. Notice the warm yellow lighting — it is intentional. Yellow light calms the nervous system. This space is designed to feel like a café, not a canteen, because the goal is for you to actually slow down, eat well, and feel at home. In a high-intensity learning environment, this kind of space matters.

entries.content: The Innovation Lab is called the Wormhole — inspired by Einstein's theory of relativity and the concept of a tunnel through space-time that accelerates movement between two points. As you walk through the entrance tunnel, the light and spatial design are meant to create a mental shift: you are leaving the regular world and entering a space dedicated entirely to building, experimentation, and accelerated learning. The wormhole, famously visualised in the film Interstellar as a three-dimensional sphere, became the conceptual model for this transition experience.
entries.short: The Wormhole is the Innovation Lab — walking in is designed to feel like a mental shift into building mode.
entries.experience_script: You are about to enter the Wormhole — Polaris's Innovation Lab. The entrance tunnel is intentional. In physics, a wormhole is a shortcut through space-time — a passage that accelerates you from one point to another faster than normal. Here, the metaphor is real. Walking through this tunnel is meant to trigger a mental shift: you are leaving passive learning behind and entering a space designed entirely for active building, experimentation, and discovery.

entries.content: The utility and service area supports the day-to-day operations of the ground floor, including the kitchen and back-of-house functions for the cafeteria.
entries.short: The utility area is the service and storage zone supporting the cafeteria and ground floor operations.

entries.content: The main corridor on the ground floor connects all zones and leads to the staircase and lift access points.
entries.short: The main corridor connects all ground floor zones.
